title: Web Development & Disciplined Inquiry (IS 117)
frameworks: CCR Loop: Create with AI → Critique with vocabulary → Revise with insight
CRC Critique: Commend, Recommend, Commend — structured peer feedback
Spell Book: accumulated precision vocabulary for human-AI communication
Context Pack: what I know, what I don't know, how I evaluate AI answers
concepts: An LLM converts energy into intelligence — vocabulary controls the efficiency
Precise vocabulary activates dense knowledge clusters; vague language scatters attention
Each named concept is a compressed knowledge packet — a key that unlocks a door in the AI's library
Shannon's information theory: information has optimal encoding, efficient encoding wins
The web is open by default — view source is a philosophy, not just a feature
Disciplined Inquiry: the habit of pausing before accepting an AI answer
The most important skill in the age of AI is knowing what to ask
Leonardo da Vinci used monastery libraries the way we use LLMs — vocabulary determines what you can see
context: IS 117 is Studio 1 of the BSEAI degree (BS in Enterprise AI — The Human Edge). It teaches web development (HTML, CSS, Git, terminal) while building the foundation skill of Disciplined Inquiry — how to formulate questions worth asking and evaluate AI-generated answers. The 40/60 split: 40% manual coding, 60% AI-assisted. Assessment: 20% quizzes, 30% participation, 50% final project + Demo Day.
